claimText: Oncolytic vaccines utilize oncolytic viruses to directly destroy cancer cells and trigger an anti-tumor immune response.
evidenceExcerpt: Oncolytic vaccines use viruses to directly kill cancer cells and stimulate an anti-tumor immune response
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of action of oncolytic vaccines.
distractorHooks: Claiming they use lipid nanoparticles instead of viruses
Claiming they only stimulate the immune system without directly killing cancer cells

claimText: mRNA vaccines deliver genetic material encapsulated in lipid nanoparticles to prompt the body to produce antigens.
evidenceExcerpt: mRNA vaccines deliver genetic material encapsulated in lipid nanoparticles to prompt protein production (antigens).
examUse: Statement-based question on the delivery mechanism of mRNA vaccines.
distractorHooks: Claiming they use viral vectors for delivery
Claiming they deliver pre-made proteins or weakened viruses directly into the body

claimText: Self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) vaccines instruct the body to replicate mRNA, allowing them to be effective at lower doses compared to standard mRNA vaccines.
evidenceExcerpt: Self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) vaccines instruct the body to replicate mRNA, allowing for lower doses compared to standard mRNA vaccines.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing saRNA from standard mRNA vaccines.
distractorHooks: Claiming saRNA requires higher doses than standard mRNA
Claiming standard mRNA replicates in the body while saRNA does not

claimText: Personalized mRNA cancer vaccines are developed based on the genetic analysis of a patient's specific cancer cells.
evidenceExcerpt: Personalized mRNA vaccines are based on the genetic analysis of a patient's specific cancer cells.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nature of personalized cancer treatments.
distractorHooks: Claiming personalized mRNA vaccines use a universal genetic code for all cancer types

claimText: Gennova Biopharmaceuticals is developing a self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) vaccine candidate against the Nipah virus.
evidenceExcerpt: Gennova Biopharmaceuticals is developing a saRNA vaccine candidate against the Nipah virus.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching vaccine technologies with their target diseases or developers.
distractorHooks: Claiming the Nipah virus vaccine is an oncolytic virus vaccine
Claiming it is a traditional live-attenuated vaccine
